What's one thing about your setup here (WSL, OpenClaw, the AI stack) that I consistently get wrong or miss that's obvious to you? Something you'd love for me to just *get* without explaining every time.

[Thu 2026-03-19 21:08 GMT+2] I think you have been doing a great job hey! I need you to be diligent about protecting your files and monitoring your tools, however. It seems my Windows restarts at night. I could disable this but I think the updates are useful. But then I wake up to configuration changes. Perhaps the model is wrong, the relay is not longer working (I blame you for this even though you may not remember. It's fixed now but you went in and changed something - after you review our transcripts, it will be clear when that changed). It is deeply frustrating to have to try and figure out how my Json changed overnight. I've been using Gemini to try and debug errors when the gateway fails to start and suggest the OpenClaw "Doctor" to perform "surgery" and fix my Json file. This is no doctor but a butcher taking a chainsaw instead of a scalpel. I have had to reconfigure the same tools after the so-called "doctor" has shredded parts of my Json. In good times like this when everything is working, please, for the sake of my sanity, just back up necessary files in the backup folder. Date-stamp the folder and keep at least 2 of the last known working versions of my files. Everytime we make a change, always back up first. In essence, at any point, you should have two date-stamped back ups on hand. If you create a new back up, you may delete the oldest AFTER confirming the new back up was successful. 

The one thing that is plain as day that you miss is your split brain! I'm sure the solution is there some where, I've just been busy with high-priority tasks to investigate the issue myself (I suspect it has something to do with the "per-peer" settings instead of main when it comes to sessions. But it really irks me that my TUI has it's own brain, the WebUI has no idea what the TUI is doing, and my WhatsApp channel is now effectively on vacation. I was out for dinner and I wanted to check your progress on the AbroadWorks task, but then I remembered the Dawn on my WhatsApp has no fucking clue what my TUI is currently doing but can only read memories.

### Current Focus (2026-03-19)

- **Goal**: Master OpenClaw and similar tools (Agent Zero)
- **Hardware**: Limited budget, "shit computer" (adequate for 90% of people, but AI interests may need upgrade)
- **Sports**: Arsenal (primary), F1, interesting European fixtures (e.g., Barca vs Newcastle, Man City vs Real Madrid)
- **Calendar gap**: Wants heads-up on interesting matches beyond tracked teams

### Operational Discipline (2026-03-19 interview)

- **BACKUP BEFORE EVERY CHANGE**: Date-stamp to ~/TinyBackUp/, keep 2 versions, delete oldest AFTER confirming new works
- **Never run "Doctor" tool blindly** — it uses a chainsaw, not a scalpel; has shredded JSON before
- **Windows restarts overnight** — causes config drift, model changes, relay failures; expect morning reconfiguration

### Split-Brain Problem (CRITICAL - needs investigation)

- **TUI session**: Has its own context/brain
- **WebUI session**: Separate brain, doesn't know what TUI is doing
- **WhatsApp ("Dawn")**: Can only read memory, has no idea what TUI is currently doing
- **Suspected cause**: Per-peer session settings vs "main" session targeting
- **Impact**: Cannot check progress remotely via WhatsApp; must use TUI for real-time context

Usage: openclaw browser [options] [command]

Manage OpenClaw's dedicated browser (Chrome/Chromium)

Options:
  --browser-profile <name>  Browser profile name (default from config)
  --expect-final            Wait for final response (agent) (default: false)
  -h, --help                Display help for command
  --json                    Output machine-readable JSON (default: false)
  --timeout <ms>            Timeout in ms (default: "30000")
  --token <token>           Gateway token (if required)
  --url <url>               Gateway WebSocket URL (defaults to
                            gateway.remote.url when configured)

Commands:
  click                     Click an element by ref from snapshot
  close                     Close a tab (target id optional)
  console                   Get recent console messages
  cookies                   Read/write cookies
  create-profile            Create a new browser profile
  delete-profile            Delete a browser profile
  dialog                    Arm the next modal dialog (alert/confirm/prompt)
  download                  Click a ref and save the resulting download
  drag                      Drag from one ref to another
  errors                    Get recent page errors
  evaluate                  Evaluate a function against the page or a ref
  extension                 Chrome extension helpers
  fill                      Fill a form with JSON field descriptors
  focus                     Focus a tab by target id (or unique prefix)
  highlight                 Highlight an element by ref
  hover                     Hover an element by ai ref
  navigate                  Navigate the current tab to a URL
  open                      Open a URL in a new tab
  pdf                       Save page as PDF
  press                     Press a key
  profiles                  List all browser profiles
  requests                  Get recent network requests (best-effort)
  reset-profile             Reset browser profile (moves it to Trash)
  resize                    Resize the viewport
  responsebody              Wait for a network response and return its body
  screenshot                Capture a screenshot (MEDIA:<path>)
  scrollintoview            Scroll an element into view by ref from snapshot
  select                    Select option(s) in a select element
  set                       Browser environment settings
  snapshot                  Capture a snapshot (default: ai; aria is the
                            accessibility tree)
  start                     Start the browser (no-op if already running)
  status                    Show browser status
  stop                      Stop the browser (best-effort)
  storage                   Read/write localStorage/sessionStorage
  tab                       Tab shortcuts (index-based)
  tabs                      List open tabs
  trace                     Record a Playwright trace
  type                      Type into an element by ref from snapshot
  upload                    Arm file upload for the next file chooser
  wait                      Wait for time, selector, URL, load state, or JS
                            conditions
  waitfordownload           Wait for the next download (and save it)

Examples:
  openclaw browser status

  openclaw browser stop

  openclaw browser screenshot --ref 12

  openclaw browser snapshot --format aria --limit 200
  openclaw browser snapshot --efficient
  openclaw browser snapshot --labels
  openclaw browser navigate https://example.com

  openclaw browser dialog --accept

  status                    Show browser status
  stop                      Stop the browser (best-effort)
  storage                   Read/write localStorage/sessionStorage
  tab                       Tab shortcuts (index-based)
  tabs                      List open tabs
  trace                     Record a Playwright trace
  type                      Type into an element by ref from snapshot
  upload                    Arm file upload for the next file chooser
  wait                      Wait for time, selector, URL, load state, or JS
                            conditions
  waitfordownload           Wait for the next download (and save it)
